issue_title: Unknown essential acr claim does not result in an error
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

**Just related to the Step up Authentication:**

Currently, the behavior in the case of an unknown essential acr claim does not comply with the OIDC specification, which states:
_If the acr claim is requested as an Essential Claim for the ID Token with a values parameter requesting specific Authentication Context Class Reference values and the implementation supports the claims parameter, the Authorization Server MUST return an acr Claim Value that matches one of the requested values. **If this is an Essential Claim and the requirement cannot be met, then the Authorization Server MUST treat that outcome as a failed authentication attempt.**_

Behavior has been removed with the implementation of the callback: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/6e4fb3a0cd539b3eb9568c3ea7c9de67a755cac6#diff-b72231a1d2641bd3ef2dfbe97433cda0a506e6c4fafa89896222339716cee6faL305

### Version

17.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

The authentication process should result in an error page instead of an successful authentication.

### Actual behavior

The authentication process results in an successful authentication.

### How to Reproduce?

To reproduce the issue, send an authentication request with an essential request and an unknown or high (e.g. 5) acr value. After finishing the authentication (entering username/password and OTP), you should receive an authentication error. Instead, you are currently authenticating successfully with the highest authentication level.

Request example:
https://{DOMAIN}/auth/realms/{REALMNAME}/protocol/openid-connect/auth?client_id={CLIENT-ID}&redirect_uri={REDIRECT-URI}&scope=openid&response_type=code&response_mode=query&nonce=exg16fxdjcu&claims=%7B%22id_token%22%3A%7B%22acr%22%3A%7B%22essential%22%3Atrue%2C%22values%22%3A%5B%22unkownvalue%22%5D%7D%7D%7D

### Anything else?

Tests for this use case are already commited:
* LevelOfAssuranceTest.java:stepupToUnknownEssentialAcrFails
* LevelOfAssuranceTest.java:essentialClaimNotReachedFails
* LevelOfAssuranceTest.java:essentialUnknownClaimFail

#### Related issue

There is another issue related to the ACR that under some circumstances, the ACR level is not correctly updated in the authentication session after finish of subflow with the ACR condition. This is possible to seen in the `LevelOfAssuranceTest.stepupAuthentication()` , which requires authentication to level 3 (push the button) in case that only the level 2 (gold) was requested.

Show error in case of an unkown essential acr claim. Make sure correc… (#10088)

* Show error in case of an unkown essential acr claim. Make sure correct acr is set after authentication flow during step-up authentication
Closes #8724

issue_title: KeycloakAuthenticatorValve (Tomcat) does not implement createRequestAuthenticator()
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Because of the missing override of createRequestAuthenticator(), the valve uses the CatalinaRequestAuthenticator instead of the TomcatRequestAuthenticator. The CatalinaRequestAuthenticator has no prevention against the session fixation attack. This security issue should be fixed as soon as possible.
see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/dd69bb5d33d0ee825fcfc2f1a0502709a60fdacf/adapters/oidc/tomcat/tomcat/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/tomcat/KeycloakAuthenticatorValve.java

### Version

9.0.3 - 15.0.2

### Expected behavior

Change of the session id after a successful login

### Actual behavior

Session id remains after login.

### How to Reproduce?

Check the session id before/after a login

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Require user to agree to 'terms and conditions' during registration
issue_body:
### Description

When a new user registers, personal data is already collected, stored and processed before the necessary consent is obtained from the user. This violates European data protection regulations and disqualifies the use of Keycloak within the scope of European law. I am astonished that so less requests on this subject have arrived. The only report I found was KEYCLOAK-8447.

Is there any workaround for this?

### Discussion

See https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-15244

issue_title: Reset Credentials Flow does not delete existing OTP
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

In the default keycloak config, the “Reset Credentials” flow contains a conditional action to “Reset OTP” when the user has 2FA enabled. That means when the user chooses “Forgot Password?” he will first need to reconfigure the OTP and after that choose a new password.

During the “Reset OTP” action, the existing authenticator should be deleted. This is not what is happening currently. Instead the existing authenticator is kept and an additional authenticator is created.

The user is not aware of this and will generally choose the same name for the authenticator as he used before. Thus he will be unable to differentiate between the old and new authenticators. I.e. after resetting his password the user will have a hard time logging in.

Even if the user was so lucky as to use a new name for the authenticator, he will be confused by having to select which authenticator to use, when one is obsolete.

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

The existing OTP credential is replaced by the new credential when resetting a password.

### Actual behavior

A new otp credential entry is added to the user.

### How to Reproduce?

Have a user with 2FA enabled and configured, then use the "Forgot Password?" function for the user.

### Anything else?

Originally reported here: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-14640
commit_message:
Improved Reset OTP authenticator (#20572)

* ResetOTP authenticator can now be configured, so that one or all existing OTP configurations are deleted upon reset.

Closes #8753
---------

issue_title: Pre-loaded sessions live forever in cache
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When offline sessions are preloaded from the database to the cache, entries have no timeout and potentially live forever.

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

When sessions are preloaded, cache timeout is set based on their expiration.

### Actual behavior

When sessions are preloaded, cache timeout is **NOT** set.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Run a Keycloak server with remote Infinispan (probably the same with embedded) and Postgres db
2. Create some offline sessions 
3. Kill Keycloak and Infinispan servers 
4. Run Infinispan and Keycloak
5. On startup Keycloak preloads offline sessions from DB to the Infinispan cache, however, the sessions seem to have no timeout and therefore live in cache forever. They are correctly removed from the database by [ClearExpiredUserSessions](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/scheduled/ClearExpiredUserSessions.java) scheduled task.

### Anything else?

Originally reported as: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-19417
This was probably introduced in this PR: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/7736
commit_message:
Single offlineSession imported in Infinispan with correctly calculated lifespan and maxIdle parameters

Close #8776

issue_title: Remove node_modules folder from the Keycloak repository
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

As discussed in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8645 it is considered bad practice to check in the `node_modules` folder in the Git repository taking into consideration that updating its dependencies may create many conflicts and make it harder to perform code reviews.

The `themes/src/main/resources/theme/keycloak/common/resources/node_modules` folder should be removed from our repository. Prior to that, we need to verify with the automation team the impact on the productization aspects. @

### Actual behavior

### How to Reproduce?

- Check the `themes/src/main/resources/theme/keycloak/common/resources/node_modules` folder

issue_title: `ui_locales` query parameter is ignored when `kc_idp_hint` is set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When trying to set the locale for KeyCloak and hint which IDP to use, KeyCloak ignores the `ui_locales` hint.

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

KeyCloak should respect the `ui_locales` hint when the user gets redirected from the IdentityProvider's site.

For example when this is the first time the user logs in, and they need to verify their e-mail address.

### Actual behavior

When the `kc_idp_hint` parameter is set KeyCloak disrespects the `ui_locales` parameter.

### How to Reproduce?

 - Start a local KeyCloak instance. Configure at least 2 locale-s in your instance (I've used English and Hungarian)
 - Configure any Identity Provider (I used google). Set the realm's settings to require email verification
 - Open the https://www.keycloak.org/app/, and enter the credentials. Click the _Save_ btn.
 - put a breakpoint in `keycloak.js` at the 502th line.
 - Append the `kc_idp_hint=google&ui_locales=hu` to the variable `url`. (Or any Locale which is not your system's locale)
 - Continue the execution
 - Continue with the login process
 - the _verify your e-mail address_ will be shown to you in your system's locale.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Keep the locale related authNotes through the IdentityBroker flow. (#10444)

issue_title: Generated delegates uses wrong class
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

These two lines: 
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f6daca8a602d5440a0564ab7eb3e654e148aba72/model/build-processor/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/processor/GenerateEntityImplementationsProcessor.java#L557-L558

```java
private final org.keycloak.models.map.common.delegate.DelegateProvider<MapClientEntityDelegate> delegateProvider;
    public MapClientEntityDelegate(org.keycloak.models.map.common.delegate.DelegateProvider<MapClientEntityDelegate> delegateProvider) {
        this.delegateProvider = delegateProvider;

```

However, we want the `delegateProvider` to provide `MapClientEntity` not another `MapClientEntityDelegate`. I think it should be the following:

```java
private final org.keycloak.models.map.common.delegate.DelegateProvider<org.keycloak.models.map.client.MapClientEntity> delegateProvider;
    public MapClientEntityDelegate(org.keycloak.models.map.common.delegate.DelegateProvider<org.keycloak.models.map.client.MapClientEntity> delegateProvider) {

```

issue_title: service-account leaking in get users API with "exact" query parameter set
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hello, 

When calling the `GET /{realm}/users` endpoint with the optional query parameter `exact` set to any value, without specifying any other query parameters, the list of users also contains service-account users from the clients with Service Accounts enabled in that realm.

It seems like this is a bug, because when this optional query parameter is not set, those service-account users are never returned.

If this is the intended behavior, I would expect to be able to replicate the default behavior by specifying `exact=true` or `exact=false` (whatever the default currently is).

In case I misunderstood something, is there a way to reliably filter out the service-account users when specifying the `exact` query parameter?

Thanks a lot for your help!

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

service-account users not showing up in the `GET /{realm}/users` endpoint, or otherwise consistent behavior when specifying or omitting the `exact` query parameter

### Actual behavior

service-account users showing up when setting the `exact` query parameter to any value

### How to Reproduce?

- create a new realm
- create a dummy OIDC client in the realm
- setting "Service Accounts Enabled" on the newly created client client
- calling `GET /{realm}/users?exact=true` or `GET /{realm}/users?exact=false`

When simply calling `GET /{realm}/users`, the service-account user does not show up

### Anything else?

_No response_

don't include service accounts in search results (#17189)

issue_title: Exact match user query by email is doing a full table scan
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

One of our internal projects needs a fast user lookup by email. Reading the API docs the GET user query with email query parameter and exact match query parameter seemed appropriate for this use case.

When tested against a user database of >2M rows the request took 13 seconds. The query was logged in the slow query log and an explain showed that it was filtering all rows. Removing the _lower_ function call from around the email in the where clause allowed it match on the index and return quickly.

We're using mysql 5.7.



### Version

11.0.2

### Expected behavior

Exact match email query should be fast.

### Actual behavior

Exact match email query took 13s to return when run against a user db of 2M rows.

### How to Reproduce?

Query a sizable user database using the Keycloak /users endpoint with the parameters ?email=<email>&exact=true.

### Anything else?

I tracked the source of this issue to the following line of code. It seems to me that the builder.lower around the email key is unnecessary since email is already forced to lower case on save. The builder.lower causes JPA to emit a lower function call in mysql syntax and the function is applied on every row prior to comparison.

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/1e1a6779befb9c836069761fb70d86cadd47b4cb/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/JpaUserProvider.java#L812
commit_message:
Improve user search performance

Removes bulder.lower() from user search queries on email and username.

Closes #8893

issue_title: keycloak.js example from the documentation leads to error path
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When I tried to implement the example from [the documentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/securing_apps/#_javascript_adapter), namely

```
<html>
<head>
    <script src="keycloak.js"></script>
    <script>
        function initKeycloak() {
            var keycloak = new Keycloak();
            keycloak.init().then(function(authenticated) {
                alert(authenticated ? 'authenticated' : 'not authenticated');
            }).catch(function() {
                alert('failed to initialize');
            });
        }
    </script>
</head>
<body onload="initKeycloak()">
    <!-- your page content goes here -->
</body>
</html>
```

I consistently reached the "failed to initialize" alert.

When printing out the error code, it was coming from [this line](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/adapters/oidc/js/src/main/resources/keycloak.js#L1361). Upon further debugging, it appears that the variable `kc.messageReceiveTimeout` is null, while the default timeout is supposed to be 10000. Studying the initialization, I came to the conclusion that it never gets initialized [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/adapters/oidc/js/src/main/resources/keycloak.js#L199-L203), since [this if-condition](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/adapters/oidc/js/src/main/resources/keycloak.js#L122) will not be fulfilled if init is not called with a non-empty options parameter. But that is what is being done in the example from the documentation.

So, this is an issue of default parameters not being initialized (or the documentation needs fixing; your team's call ;-))

### Version

15.0.1

### Expected behavior

I would have expected that default values are set no matter what is passed to the init function.

### Actual behavior

As mentioned in the description, my code is reaching the `catch` statement.

### How to Reproduce?

Just use your example as is from the documentation, point it to an OIDC configured client, and you should be able to reproduce.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: port in location header is -1 when doing SAML global logout
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When doing SP-initiated SAML global logout, the port in location header to redirect to logoutUrl of the SAML adapter is -1.
Suppose there are EAP/Wildfly with SAML adapter and Apache httpd as a reverse proxy in front of EAP/Wildfly, and their listen ports are 8080 and 80.

When a browser access httpd, the Host header of its request has no port number like "Host: localhost". Forwarding the request to EAP/Wildfly, the host header would be "Host: localhost" as well. Then, URI.getPort() returns -1, because no port is specified in the host header.

In KeycloakHttpServerAuthenticationMechanism#sendRedirect():
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/b03b390dd2475979d97f34891b96839a68cd7097/adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/KeycloakHttpServerAuthenticationMechanism.java#L176
exchange.getURI().getPort() returns -1 in this case, and it makes location header invalid like:

http://localhost:-1/app-profile/saml/logout.jsp


### Version

RH-SSO 7.4.x

### Expected behavior

Location header should be like:
http://localhost:80/app-profile/saml/logout.jsp
or
http://localhost/app-profile/saml/logout.jsp


### Actual behavior

http://localhost:-1/app-profile/saml/logout.jsp


### How to Reproduce?

do GLO

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Blacklist false positive rate could be set a lot lower.
issue_body:
### Description

Your blacklist feature uses a bloom filter, a probabilistic data structure that is super efficient at answering "Is this password in this really big set of bad passwords" at the cost of the occasional false positive. Yay!
 
Keycloak sets [1] that false positive rate at 0.01. That means one in a hundred times you set a password it will be rejected as "blacklisted" no matter how long and crazy it is. Boo! My users with "stupidly good" passwords, like the auto generated ones from password managers, get scared when they see that their password has been blacklisted.

Bloom filters are like suuuuper efficient, and you could add a lot of zeros to that (0.00001 for example) for really not much [2] memory at all.

Please stop scaring my users that are smart enough to choose good passwords, but not as smart as Burton Howard Bloom.

1. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/bfce612641a70e106b20b136431f0e4046b5c37f/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/policy/BlacklistPasswordPolicyProviderFactory.java#L190

2. https://hur.st/bloomfilter/?n=200000&p=.000000001&m=&k=

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

_No response_

### Details

Literally changing 0.01 to 0.00001 or something. I mean, check my math because we don't wanna eat ALL THE RAM
commit_message:
Revise blacklist password policy provider #8982

- Reduce false positive probability from 1% to 0.01% to avoid
rejecting to many actually good passwords.
- Make false positive rate configurable via spi config
- Revised log messages

Supported syntax variant:
`passwordBlacklist(wordlistFilename)`

Fixes #8982

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Access Token claims not imported using OpenID Connect v1.0 Identity Provider Attribute Importer Mappers
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

Hi there !

We have configured an external OIDC Identity Provider (type: `OpenID Connect v1.0`), and are trying to map claims present in the IDP Access Token to Keycloak User Attributes, using an Attribute Importer Mapper.

But Keycloak seems unable to find the claims in the Access Token, with the following DEBUG messages reported:

```
DEBUG [org.keycloak.broker.oidc.mappers.AbstractJsonUserAttributeMapper] (default task-6) Going to process JsonNode path role-coh-irn71429 on data null
DEBUG [org.keycloak.broker.oidc.mappers.AbstractJsonUserAttributeMapper] (default task-6) Going to process JsonNode path iat on data null
```



### Version

10.0.1, but also reproducible with 15.0.2

### Expected behavior

The tooltip message on the Mapper type states:

```
Import declared claim if it exists in ID, access token or the claim set 
returned by the user profile endpoint into the specified user property or attribute.
```

Using the documentation provided [here](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#retrieving-external-idp-tokens), I managed to retrieve the external IDP Token, and can confirm it contains the expected claim. 
So I would actually expect Keycloak to resolve the claims present in the Access Token.

### Actual behavior

The user created in Keycloak at first login does not contain the attribute expected.

### How to Reproduce?

- Configure Keycloak by adding an Identity Provider of type `OpenID Connect v1.0` : https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_identity_broker_oidc
- Configure the external IDP to include a claim in the Access Token only
- In Keycloak, add an Attribute Importer mapper in the identity provider configured. This Attribute Importer should map the claim present in the Access Token
- Try to login using this new Identity Broker. Authentication should pass, but in Keycloak, the user attributes will not contain the mapped claim.

By the way, to make things clearer, I added two test cases in the [following branch in my fork](https://github.com/rm3l/keycloak/tree/claims_not_imported_using_oidc_idp_attribute_mappers_test). 

You can run these tests with the command below:
```shell
mvn -f testsuite/integration-arquillian/pom.xml clean install \
  -Dtest=KcOidcAccessTokenOnlyClaimsUserAttributeMapperTest,OidcAccessTokenOnlyClaimsUserAttributeMapperTest
```

`KcOidcAccessTokenOnlyClaimsUserAttributeMapperTest`, which passes, registers a `keycloak-oidc` Identity Provider.
But as reported here, `OidcAccessTokenOnlyClaimsUserAttributeMapperTest` does not pass because it registers an `oidc` IDP.



### Anything else?

Few months ago, I opened a discussion [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/8462) about that issue,but got no response so far. After some discussions, I am finally about to contribute a PR for this issue.

While the reproduction steps are a bit different, @galzetta reported a similar issue (#8961) with similar debug logs. I could not add a test case for this other issue, so I am not sure the linked PR will fix that issue.

Closes #9004

issue_title: Update client with registration access token gained by client registration
issue_body:
### Describe the bug


When we try to modify a client with a registration access  token token, the client is changed, but the changes do not take into account the authorizationSettings part of the clientRepresentation. 

### Version

15.0.2

### Expected behavior

When creating client with registration access token.
- authorizationSettings is appart from ClientRepresentation, so it should not be ignoring in the update response
- authorizationSettings is appart from ClientRepresentation, so it should be visible in the creation, update, response of client 



### Actual behavior

- authorizationSettings in the PUT JSON is not producing error 
- if we `authorizationSettings` with typo like `authorizationSettiIIIIIIIngs`, the PUT is producing a error (invalid JSON)- 
- authorizationSettings in the PUT JSON is ignoring in the update process.
- the success response does not contain anything related to authz settings

### How to Reproduce?

Suppose my keycloak server is at https://keycloak.local

First create a client with client default registration on the URI https://keycloak.local/auth/realms/myrealm/clients-registrations/default passing clientId in the POST body.

Then retrieve the registrationAccessToken in the response.

Then, according to doc (https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-documentation/blob/main/securing_apps/topics/client-registration.adoc)  
We may update the client by passing a ClientRepresentation and using registrationAccessToken.
For exemple change the `allowRemoteResourceManagement` to `false`

Exemple in python 
```python
import requests

# Initial token created in the UI
initial_token = "xxxxx"

client_id = "new_client"

data = {"clientId": client_id}

# The first time we use intial token.
headers = {
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
    "Authorization": "bearer {}".format(initial_token)
}

# Creating the client
resp = requests.post(https://keycloak.local/auth/realms/myrealm/clients-registrations/default, headers=headers, verify=False, data=data)

# New Bearer  with registration access token coming from response
headers["Authorization"] = "bearer {}" .format(resp.json()["registrationAccessToken"])

# We want to turn off allowRemoteResourceManagement (default to true)
data = json.dumps({
"authorizationSettings": {"allowRemoteResourceManagement": False}
})

resp = requests.put(https://keycloak.local/auth/realms/myrealm/clients-registrations/default, headers=headers, verify=False, data)

```` 
No change in the keycloak authorization settings in the UI....

Why ? Isn't the bevahiour of "SELF" registration to be COMPLETE ? We should auto register client without use of admin to change authorization settings. Specifically in context of microservices who want to register theme self and telling keycloak what ressources endpoints they actually process.

issue_title: Configuration of custom JDBC driver is broken (leads to Azure SQL cannot be used as database)
issue_body:
Configuration of custom JDBC driver is broken (leads to Azure SQL cannot be used as database)

In previous versions of keycloak.x it was possible to configure the JDBC driver by settings the environment variable _KC_DB_DRIVER_ .
This is e.g. required to run keycloak.x in Azure using Azure SQL (see also [disussion on dev mailing list](https://groups.google.com/g/keycloak-dev/c/ZGuX0CFWqwo/m/uEHToZDSBwAJ)).

With keycloak.x 15.1.0 this does no longer work - the database driver is now set based on the db settings to a hard coded driver class. 
In case of mssql this uses a driver that uses XA transactions which are not supported by Azure SQL.
When trying to set the jdbc driver via configuration, keycloak uses the H2 database driver always.

I added a unit test to show the behaviour to [ConfigurationTest.java](https://github.com/bosch-io/keycloak/blob/bug/keycloak.x-setting-jdbc-driver/quarkus/runtime/src/test/java/org/keycloak/provider/quarkus/ConfigurationTest.java#L257).

Has this been changed intentionally or is this a bug?

_Originally posted by @ioemat in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/8654#discussioncomment-1799232_

issue_title: Ternary conditional operator uses primitive type in some cases, it could throw NPEs
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

There are some place where we use conditional ternary operator like this:

`return (res == null) ? origScope.getLong(key, defaultValue) : Long.parseLong(res);`

This could throw NPE when `res == null` and  `origScope.getLong(key, defaultValue)` returns also `null` The explanation for this behavior could be found here:  https://docs.oracle.com/javase/specs/jls/se7/html/jls-15.html#jls-15.25 

Specifically this sentence: `If one of the second and third operands is of primitive type T, and the type of the other is the result of applying boxing conversion (§5.1.7) to T, then the type of the conditional expression is T.` 

### Version

15.1.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: PAR logic affecting /auth endpoint
issue_body:
### Description

In KC 15 we have been introduced with unexpected breaking behavior. In my opinion logic in PAR draft that has been reference and implemented here [KEYCLOAK-18729](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/8262) should only affect PAR endpoint as defined in the draft.
Currently this logic [in code](https://github.com/pedroigor/keycloak/blob/da0a0d0fdf1d2921d42b04b451bce4d1452be969/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/request/AuthzEndpointRequestObjectParser.java#L105) affects auth endpoint and is breaking [OIDC JWTRequests](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#JWTRequests)
 `When the request parameter is used, the OpenID Connect request parameter values contained in the JWT supersede those passed using the OAuth 2.0 request syntax. However, parameters MAY also be passed using the OAuth 2.0 request syntax even when a Request Object is used; this would typically be done to enable a cached, pre-signed (and possibly pre-encrypted) Request Object value to be used containing the fixed request parameters, while parameters that can vary with each request, such as state and nonce, are passed as OAuth 2.0 parameters. `
With this new implementation not passed values from request object override OAuth 2.0 request syntax and we get get error for invalid request.
commit_message:
PAR logic affecting /auth endpoint

Closes #9289

issue_title: Keep track of transactions in MapStorage
issue_body:
In the `MapStorage` implementations, a new transaction is returned everytime a `createTransaction` is called, even if it is called multiple times in the same session. There is an attempt to reuse it in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/96b2669a00dc1396138f863bbc11aa0b0aa3f0b6/model/map/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/storage/chm/ConcurrentHashMapStorage.java#L131
However this attempt is never complemented by putting the transaction into the session, so it is futile. Other map storages just return always a new instance.
Fix to return the same instance per map storage per session.
